title: Drip vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Omnisend: pricing, features, and ratings. Find the best email marketing platform for your ecommerce business.
h1: Drip vs Omnisend: Detailed Comparison for Ecommerce
intro: Both Drip and Omnisend are purpose-built email marketing platforms targeting ecommerce businesses, but they take different approaches to pricing and features. This comparison will help you determine which platform offers better value and functionality for your specific needs.
choose_a_if: You need enterprise-grade ecommerce tracking and revenue attribution
Deep integration with your store's backend is a priority
You want the strongest affiliate program (15% recurring commission)
choose_b_if: You're budget-conscious and want to start free with up to 250 contacts
You need omnichannel campaigns across email, SMS, and push notifications together
Customer support quality matters—Omnisend has significantly higher Trustpilot ratings (4.6 vs 3.5)

faq.a: It depends on your priorities. Drip excels at ecommerce-specific features like revenue attribution and store integration, making it ideal for larger operations needing detailed tracking. Omnisend is better for small-to-medium businesses seeking affordability, ease of use, and omnichannel capabilities, with notably higher customer satisfaction scores.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Omnisend?
faq.a: Omnisend is significantly cheaper. It offers a free tier supporting up to 250 contacts, then starts at $16/month for paid plans. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. For startups and small businesses, Omnisend provides better value.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email list exports and imports. Most ecommerce email platforms allow you to export your contacts and automation data. However, you'll need to manually recreate complex automations in the new platform, as workflow structures differ between tools.
